title: Power of Two — O(1) Bit Trick
description:
Return true if n is a positive power of two (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, …), false otherwise.

**The point**: a naive solution loops dividing by 2 (O(log n)). A bit-manipulation trick does it in **O(1)**: a power of two has exactly one bit set.
hints: First make sure n is positive — 0 and negatives can't be powers of two.
Write a few powers of two in binary: 1, 10, 100, 1000. Each has exactly ONE bit set.
n & (n-1) clears the lowest set bit. If that result is 0 and n is positive, n had only one bit set.

testCases.description: negatives are not powers of two
